question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause RetrieveGenesis(ctx) to return a block with index 0 and hash matching a different network's genesis, causing clients to believe they are on the wrong network and refuse to submit transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that CurrentBlockTimestamp is returned as milliseconds from currentBlock.GetTimestampMillis() with no upper bound check, causing clients that use this timestamp for sync decisions to believe the network is far ahead and stop syncing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the context passed to addressBookEntryRepo.Entries(ctx) to be cancelled mid-query, causing the DB to return a partial result set that is silently treated as a complete peer list, causing clients to receive an incomplete view of the network topology?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that NetworkOptions() builds operationStatuses by iterating over types.GetTransactionResults() without any size limit, and if that map is large, the response payload causes HTTP clients to time out and fail to discover valid operation types, partitioning them from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that errors.Errors is a global mutable slice appended to at init time, and if a race condition exists during initialization, NetworkOptions() could return a partial or corrupted error list that causes Rosetta clients to misclassify network errors and stop retrying, partitioning them from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that trigger the ErrNodeAccountIdsEmpty error (retriable: true, code 140), causing Rosetta clients to endlessly retry /network/status and never fall back to an alternative endpoint, effectively partitioning them from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the service returns the same n.network identifier in both NetworkList() and NetworkStatus() without any freshness validation, causing clients that cache the network identifier to continue using a stale one after a network upgrade, partitioning them from the new network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause both addressBook101 and addressBook102 queries in Entries() to return empty results, causing NetworkStatus() to return a valid response with an empty Peers list, making all Rosetta clients believe the network has no nodes and stop routing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the latestNodeServiceEndpoints SQL query uses string_agg to concatenate endpoints with commas, and if an endpoint string itself contains a comma, the split in toAddressBookEntry() will produce malformed endpoint entries that cause clients to fail to connect to nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that peers.ToRosetta() formats PeerID as fmt.Sprintf('%d', e.NodeId) without any validation of NodeId, and if NodeId is 0 or negative, clients may reject the peer entry and fail to discover valid network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause RetrieveLatest(ctx) to return a block whose GetRosettaBlockIdentifier() returns a nil or empty hash, causing Rosetta clients to fail block validation and refuse to sync with the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that NetworkStatus() does not check whether CurrentBlockIdentifier.Index >= GenesisBlockIdentifier.Index, allowing a scenario where a DB inconsistency causes the current block to appear before genesis, causing clients to reject the response and consider the network partitioned?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests at a rate that causes the defer cancel() in addressBookEntryRepo.Entries() to fire before the DB query completes, causing the query to be cancelled and returning ErrDatabaseError, making the network appear unreachable to all clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkOptions()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that NetworkOptions() returns HistoricalBalanceLookup: true unconditionally, causing clients to attempt historical balance queries that exhaust DB resources and prevent NetworkStatus() from returning peer information?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the DB to return a node with an empty Endpoints string, which toAddressBookEntry() converts to an empty []string{}, causing clients to receive a peer with no endpoints and fail to connect to that node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the service does not validate the format of IP:port endpoint strings returned by the DB before including them in the Peers response, causing clients to receive malformed endpoints that prevent them from connecting to network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the coalesce in latestNodeServiceEndpoints to return NULL for all endpoints, resulting in empty endpoint strings for all peers, causing clients to receive a peer list with no usable connection information?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the address book query joins on max(start_consensus_timestamp) without any recency check, causing the service to return a very old address book (e.g., from months ago) as the current peer list, partitioning clients from nodes that have since changed their endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the left join in latestNodeServiceEndpoints to return NULL for node account IDs (when node n has no matching record), causing coalesce(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id) to fall back to a potentially stale account ID that clients use for routing?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that NetworkStatus() does not include any OldestBlockIdentifier in its response, causing Rosetta clients that require this field to fail validation and refuse to use the service for network discovery?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause RetrieveGenesis(ctx) and RetrieveLatest(ctx) to return the same block (when the chain has only one block), causing clients to compute a zero-length chain and believe the network has not started?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the service does not validate that n.network is non-nil before returning it in NetworkList(), and if the network identifier is nil due to a misconfiguration, clients receive a nil network identifier and cannot route any requests?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the DB to return duplicate node IDs in the address book query (due to the group by not being unique), causing clients to receive duplicate peer entries and potentially connect to the same node multiple times while missing other nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by exploiting the fact that the service does not validate the n.version field before returning it in NetworkOptions(), and if version is nil, clients receive a nil version and fail to validate the network options response?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/network_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: NetworkStatus()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition by sending requests that cause the DB context cancellation (defer cancel()) to race with the DB query in Entries(), causing intermittent ErrDatabaseError responses that make the network appear flapping and cause clients to mark it as partitioned?
